libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: For JVM tests that exercise method interception, proxy generation, or embedded CGLIB internals, configure the test JVM with `--add-opens=java.base/java.lang=ALL-UNNAMED` and pass the same flag to native-image build arguments. Basic injector/binding coverage works with the normal transitive dependencies only. For 2.9.1, do not reuse 2.1.7 imports for internal utility/CGLIB classes: the jarjar-relocated internal class simple names are `$`-prefixed, while the public `com.google.inject` API remains the safer target.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8858 [Automation] javac compile fails for org.sonatype.sisu:sisu-guice:2.9.1; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015

risks: The reported javac failure is caused by tests importing internal classes that existed without `$` prefixes in 2.1.7 but are `$`-prefixed in 2.9.1, not by a missing external dependency.
The POM declares `org.slf4j:slf4j-api` as optional; it should not be added unless tests intentionally cover SLF4J-specific behavior.
AOP/proxy tests can pass compilation but fail at runtime on JDK 17+ without the `java.lang` module open.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: No extra Maven dependency or Docker image is needed, but meaningful Guice AOP/proxy coverage on modern JDKs requires opening java.lang for the embedded CGLIB code.
